query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "evopolicygym: evaluating autonomous policy evolution in interactive en" computer science
rationale: The candidate paper focuses on "Resource Abstraction" and "reward shaping" for Multiagent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) in congestion problems, which does not address the user's specific question about "EvoPolicyGym" (an autonomous policy evolution framework) or the extension of that specific evaluation environment. While both involve autonomous agents, the paper lacks the specific mechanism (policy evolution/autonomous policy generation), the specific benchmark (EvoPolicyGym), or the foundation
